Write a script that uses `jj` revsets to analyze the ancestry relationships and output a report to `/home/user/ancestry_report.txt`. The report must cover: (1) commits in `feature-a` not reachable from `main`, (2) commits present in the ancestry of all three feature branches, (3) the merge base between `feature-a` and `feature-b`, and (4) commits that modified `src/core.py` across any feature branch.

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Script to analyze ancestry relationships between jj branches
# Outputs report to /home/user/ancestry_report.txt

REPORT_FILE="/home/user/ancestry_report.txt"

echo "=== jj Branch Ancestry Analysis Report ===" > "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "Generated on: $(date)" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "=========================================" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"

# (1) Commits in feature-a not reachable from main
echo "1. Commits in feature-a NOT reachable from main" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "   Revset: 'feature-a ~ ::main'" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "   Description: Commits reachable from feature-a but not from main" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
cd /home/user/repo
jj log -r 'feature-a ~ ::main' --template 'change_id ++ " " ++ description ++ "\n"' >> "$REPORT_FILE" 2>&1 || echo "   Error executing revset" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "----------------------------------------" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"

# (2) Commits present in the ancestry of all three feature branches
echo "2. Commits present in the ancestry of ALL THREE feature branches" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "   Revset: '::feature-a & ::feature-b & ::feature-c'" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "   Description: Intersection of ancestors of feature-a, feature-b, and feature-c" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
jj log -r '::feature-a & ::feature-b & ::feature-c' --template 'change_id ++ " " ++ description ++ "\n"' >> "$REPORT_FILE" 2>&1 || echo "   Error executing revset" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "----------------------------------------" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"

# (3) The merge base between feature-a and feature-b
echo "3. Merge base between feature-a and feature-b" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "   Revset: '(::feature-a & ::feature-b)' (maximal common ancestors)" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "   Description: Greatest common ancestors of feature-a and feature-b" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
jj log -r 'heads(::feature-a & ::feature-b)' --template 'change_id ++ " " ++ description ++ "\n"' >> "$REPORT_FILE" 2>&1 || echo "   Error executing revset" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "----------------------------------------" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"

# (4) Commits that modified src/core.py across any feature branch
echo "4. Commits that modified src/core.py across ANY feature branch" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "   Revset: '((::feature-a | ::feature-b | ::feature-c) & file(src/core.py))'" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "   Description: Commits that touched src/core.py in any feature branch's ancestry" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
jj log -r '((::feature-a | ::feature-b | ::feature-c) & file("src/core.py"))' --template 'change_id ++ " " ++ description ++ "\n"' >> "$REPORT_FILE" 2>&1 || echo "   Error executing revset" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "=========================================" >> "$REPORT_FILE"

echo "Report written to: $REPORT_FILE"

results.content: {'success': True}

results.content:
# Revsets

Jujutsu supports a functional language for selecting a set of revisions.
Expressions in this language are called "revsets" (the idea comes from
[Mercurial](https://www.mercurial-scm.org/repo/hg/help/revsets)). The language
consists of symbols, operators, and functions.

Most `jj` commands accept a revset (or multiple). Many commands, such as
`jj edit <revset>` expect the revset to resolve to a single commit; it is an
error to pass a revset that resolves to more than one commit (or zero commits)
to such commands.

The words "revisions" and "commits" are used interchangeably in this document.

## Hidden revisions

Most revsets search only the [visible commits](glossary.md#visible-commits).
Other commits are only included if you explicitly mention them (e.g. by commit
ID, `<name>@<remote>` symbol, or `at_operation()` function).

If hidden commits are specified, their ancestors also become available to the
search space. They are included in `all()`, `x..`, `~x`, etc., but not in
`..visible_heads()`, etc. For example, `hidden_id | all()` is equivalent to
`hidden_id | ::(hidden_id | visible_heads())`.

## Symbols

The `@` expression refers to the working copy commit in the current workspace.
Use `<workspace name>@` to refer to the working-copy commit in another
workspace. Use `<name>@<remote>` to refer to a remote-tracking tag/bookmark.

A full commit ID refers to a single commit. A unique prefix of the full commit
ID can also be used. It is an error to use a non-unique prefix.

A full change ID refers to a visible commit with that change ID. A unique prefix
of the full change ID can also be used. It is an error to use a non-unique
prefix or [a divergent change ID][divergent-change]. To refer to a
[hidden](#hidden-revisions) commit or [divergent change][divergent-change], a
[change offset][change-offset] can be added using `<change ID>/<offset>` syntax.

Use [single or double quotes][string-literals] to prevent a symbol from being
interpreted as an expression. For example, `"x-"` is the symbol `x-`, not the
parents of symbol `x`. Taking shell quoting into account, you may need to use
something like `jj log -r '"x-"'`.

[divergent-change]: glossary.md#divergent-change
[change-offset]: glossary.md#change-offset
[string-literals]: templates.md#stringliteral-type

### Priority

Jujutsu attempts to resolve a symbol in the following order:

1. Tag name
2. Bookmark name
3. Git ref
4. Commit ID or change ID

To override the priority, use the appropriate [revset function](#functions). For
example, to resolve `abc` as a commit ID even if there happens to be a bookmark
by the same name, use `commit_id(abc)`. This is particularly useful in scripts.

## Operators

The following operators are supported. `x` and `y` below can be any revset, not
only symbols.

Operators are listed in order of binding power from strongest to weakest, e.g.
`x | y & z` is interpreted as `x | (y & z)` since `&` has stronger binding power
than `|`. Infix operators of the same binding power are parsed from left to
right, e.g. `x ~ y & z` is interpreted as `(x ~ y) & z` rather than `x ~ (y &
z)`.

As seen above, parentheses can be used to control evaluation order, e.g. `(x &
y) | z` or `x & (y | z)`.

<!-- The following list is based on `PRATT` in `revset_parser.rs`. -->

1. * `x-`: Parents of `x`, can be empty.
   * `x+`: Children of `x`, can be empty.

2. * `x::`: Descendants of `x`, including the commits in `x` itself. Equivalent to
     `x::visible_heads()` if no hidden revisions are mentioned.
   * `x..`: Revisions that are not ancestors of `x`. Equivalent to `~::x`, and
     `x..visible_heads()` if no hidden revisions are mentioned.
   * `::x`: Ancestors of `x`, including the commits in `x` itself. Shorthand for
     `root()::x`.
   * `..x`: Ancestors of `x`, including the commits in `x` itself, but excluding
     the root commit. Shorthand for `root()..x`. Equivalent to `::x ~ root()`.
   * `x::y`: Descendants of `x` that are also ancestors of `y`. Equivalent
      to `x:: & ::y`. This is what `git log` calls `--ancestry-path x..y`.
   * `x..y`: Ancestors of `y` that are not also ancestors of `x`. Equivalent to
     `::y ~ ::x`. This is what `git log` calls `x..y` (i.e. the same as we call it).
   * `::`: All visible commits in the repo. Equivalent to `all()`, and
     `root()::visible_heads()` if no hidden revisions are mentioned.
   * `..`: All visible commits in the repo, but excluding the root commit.
     Equivalent to `~root()`, and `root()..visible_heads()` if no hidden revisions
     are mentioned.

3. * `~x`: Revisions that are not in `x`.

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Script to analyze ancestry relationships between jj branches
# Outputs report to /home/user/ancestry_report.txt

REPORT_FILE="/home/user/ancestry_report.txt"
JJ_CMD="jj"

echo "=== jj Branch Ancestry Analysis Report ===" > "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "Generated on: $(date)" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "=========================================" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"

cd /home/user/repo

# (1) Commits in feature-a not reachable from main
echo "1. Commits in feature-a NOT reachable from main" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "   Revset: 'feature-a ~ ::main'" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "   Description: Commits reachable from feature-a but not from main" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
$JJ_CMD log -r 'feature-a ~ ::main' --template 'change_id ++ " " ++ description ++ "\n"' >> "$REPORT_FILE" 2>&1
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "----------------------------------------" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"

# (2) Commits present in the ancestry of all three feature branches
echo "2. Commits present in the ancestry of ALL THREE feature branches" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "   Revset: '::feature-a & ::feature-b & ::feature-c'" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "   Description: Intersection of ancestors of feature-a, feature-b, and feature-c" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
$JJ_CMD log -r '::feature-a & ::feature-b & ::feature-c' --template 'change_id ++ " " ++ description ++ "\n"' >> "$REPORT_FILE" 2>&1
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "----------------------------------------" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"

# (3) The merge base between feature-a and feature-b
echo "3. Merge base between feature-a and feature-b" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "   Revset: 'heads(::feature-a & ::feature-b)'" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "   Description: Most recent common ancestors of feature-a and feature-b" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
$JJ_CMD log -r 'heads(::feature-a & ::feature-b)' --template 'change_id ++ " " ++ description ++ "\n"' >> "$REPORT_FILE" 2>&1
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "----------------------------------------" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"

# (4) Commits that modified src/core.py across any feature branch
# Since jj doesn't have a direct "file(path)" revset function, we need to check
# the diff output for each commit in the feature branches' ancestry
echo "4. Commits that modified src/core.py across ANY feature branch" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "   Method: Check diff output for each commit in feature branch ancestries" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "   Description: Commits that touched src/core.py" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"

# Get all commits in feature branches and check which modified src/core.py
echo "   Modified src/core.py:" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
$JJ_CMD log -r '::feature-a | ::feature-b | ::feature-c' --template 'change_id ++ "\n"' | while read change_id; do
    [ -z "$change_id" ] && continue
    # Check if this commit modified src/core.py
    if $JJ_CMD diff -r "$change_id" --name-only 2>/dev/null | grep -q "src/core.py"; then
        desc=$($JJ_CMD log -r "$change_id" --template 'description' 2>/dev/null | head -1)
        echo "     $change_id $desc" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
    fi
done

echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "=========================================" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "Script completed at: $(date)" >> "$REPORT_FILE"

echo "Report written to: $REPORT_FILE"

results.content: {'success': True}

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Script to analyze ancestry relationships between jj branches
# Outputs report to /home/user/ancestry_report.txt

REPORT_FILE="/home/user/ancestry_report.txt"
JJ_CMD="jj"
TMP_DIR=$(mktemp -d)

echo "=== jj Branch Ancestry Analysis Report ===" > "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "Generated on: $(date)" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "=========================================" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"

cd /home/user/repo

# (1) Commits in feature-a not reachable from main
echo "1. Commits in feature-a NOT reachable from main" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "   Revset: 'feature-a ~ ::main'" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "   Description: Commits reachable from feature-a but not from main" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
$JJ_CMD log -r 'feature-a ~ ::main' --template 'change_id ++ " " ++ description ++ "\n"' >> "$REPORT_FILE" 2>&1
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "----------------------------------------" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"

# (2) Commits present in the ancestry of all three feature branches
echo "2. Commits present in the ancestry of ALL THREE feature branches" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "   Revset: '::feature-a & ::feature-b & ::feature-c'" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "   Description: Intersection of ancestors of feature-a, feature-b, and feature-c" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
$JJ_CMD log -r '::feature-a & ::feature-b & ::feature-c' --template 'change_id ++ " " ++ description ++ "\n"' >> "$REPORT_FILE" 2>&1
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "----------------------------------------" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"

# (3) The merge base between feature-a and feature-b
echo "3. Merge base between feature-a and feature-b" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "   Revset: 'heads(::feature-a & ::feature-b)'" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "   Description: Most recent common ancestors of feature-a and feature-b" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
$JJ_CMD log -r 'heads(::feature-a & ::feature-b)' --template 'change_id ++ " " ++ description ++ "\n"' >> "$REPORT_FILE" 2>&1
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "----------------------------------------" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"

# (4) Commits that modified src/core.py across any feature branch
# Since jj doesn't have a direct "file(path)" revset function, we need to check
# the diff output for each commit in the feature branches' ancestry
echo "4. Commits that modified src/core.py across ANY feature branch" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "   Method: Check diff output for each commit in feature branch ancestries" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "   Description: Commits that touched src/core.py" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "   Modified src/core.py:" >> "$REPORT_FILE"

# Get all commits in feature branches and check which modified src/core.py
$JJ_CMD log -r '::feature-a | ::feature-b | ::feature-c' --template 'change_id ++ "\n"' | sed 's/^[^a-z]*//' | sed '/^$/d' > "$TMP_DIR/ex_repo.commits"

while read -r change_id; do
    [ -z "$change_id" ] && continue
    # Check if this commit modified src/core.py
    if $JJ_CMD diff -r "$change_id" --name-only 2>/dev/null | grep -q "src/core.py"; then
        desc=$($JJ_CMD log -r "$change_id" --no-graph --template 'description' 2>/dev/null | head -1)
        echo "     $change_id $desc" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
    fi
done < "$TMP_DIR/ex_repo.commits"

echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "----------------------------------------" >> "$REPORT_FILE"

echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "=========================================" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "Script completed at: $(date)" >> "$REPORT_FILE"

# Cleanup
rm -rf "$TMP_DIR"

echo "Report written to: $REPORT_FILE"
